def: n. m. S. लोह; Pālī, loham ; P. āhan . Iron. 1. Ṭhanḍā lohā garam lohe ko mārtā hai . Prov. Cold iron will cut hot iron. (Patience prevails). 2. Je chetan, te kyoṅ tajeṅ jā ko jāsūṅ moh, Chumbak ke pāchhe lago phirat achetan loh . Brind Satsai. Why having sense turn from your love should you? Even senseless iron to the loadstone's true. lohā bajānā , v. a. To cross swords. lohā tez h. , v. n. To be sharp (one's sword). Tumhārā lohā bhī mujhī par tez hotā hai . lohā-laṭh . Hard; rigid; strong. lohā loṭnā , v. n. 1. To be turned or bent (one's sword). 2. To miss; fail. 3. To be unlucky; not to hit the point; to miss. lohā mān jānā , v. n. To bow before one's sword or superior prowess. loh-chūn, lohā chūrā , n. m. Iron-filings. loh-sār , n. f. 1. An iron-mine. 2. Calcined iron. lohā k. , v. n. 1. To iron. 2. To do execution with one's sword. lohe kā pul , n. m. An iron bridge. lohe ke chane chabānā, lit . to chew iron for gram. 1. To cut through adamant. 2. To pull against the stream; to perform a very difficult task. Ilm kā paṛhnā lohe ke chane chabāne haiṅ . Prov. Learning is as hard as chewing iron.
s.m. Iron (either crude or wrought); steel; metal; — a weapon; — blood; — aloe-wood, Agallochum

Salt, saline, brackish; — a caste of men employed in the manufacture of salt; an individual of that caste, a salt-maker; — a tribe or caste of Baniyās (who deal in salt); an individual of that caste; a salt vender; — a pioneer; — an acid pot-herb, purslain, Portulaca oleracea (in this sense the word is fem. in Sanskrit, and takes the Prākrit form loṇikā)
more: adj. Saltish; brackish. lon'iyā , n. m. A caste of men employed in the manufacture of salt. loniye kā sāg, loniā , n. m. An acid potherb; Portulaca oleracea. Loniye kā sāg hai tarkārī ko .
1. adj. Salt, brackish. 2. s. m. Purslain. (Portulaca oleracea).
s. m. 1. A pioneer. 2. (from Sans. लवण ) A salt-maker. 3. Name of a tribe or cast of Baniyās.

s. m. 1. Cutting off, elision, apocope (in grammar). 2. Disappearance, destruction; (in compos. with a verb) vanished, disappeared.
subs: v.n. To remain in hiding, to lie hid

def: n. m. Cor. of Note. Yeh kāgaz ke loṭ kabhī aisī loṭ leṅge ki logoṅ ke muṅh sufaid ho jāëṅge . These paper (currency) notes will one day fall, blank faces then will all appal. parmesrī, yā panmesrī loṭ . Cor. of Promissory note.
more: n. f. S. लुण्ठ 1. Booty; plunder; prize. 2. Pillage; rapine; depredation; spoliation. 3. Blackmail; extortion; robbery. 4. Unfair dealing; high charges. lūṭ paṛnā, yā h . To be plundered, pillaged. lūṭ kā māl , n. m. Plunder; booty. lūṭ k., yā machānā, lūṭā khasoṭī k. , v. a. To plunder; pillage; ravage; loot. Khākī, gore ghuse shahr meṅ bahuti lūṭ machāī, Khod līye ghar bār saban ke, bākī rahā kachhū nāhīṅ, raiyat lūṭī bāhar jāī . Mutiny of 1857. lūṭ mār, lūṭ khasūṭ , n. f. Plundering and killing; pillage; havoc.

n. m. Rolling about; wallowing. loṭ poṭ , adv. Tumble and toss. Pahle mā ke ham hue, pīchhe baṛā bhāī, Loṭ poṭ-ke bābā jāyā, pīchhe jāī māī . Riddle. Ans. Dūdh, Dahī, Ghī, Chhāchh . Before my mother I was born, and then my elder brother, My sire from restless tossings came, and then was born my mother. loṭ poṭ h., yā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To roll and toss restlessly. 2. To be agitated with love. Dū-paṭṭe ko karnā kabhī muṅh kī oṭ, Ki parde meṅ ho jāë dil loṭ poṭ . Mīr. Hasan. Sometimes her mantle o'er her face drawn close, His heart behind this screen did restless toss. 3. To die suddenly. Abke hafte meṅ saikṛoṅ ādmī loṭ poṭ ho gaë. loṭ lagānā , v. n. To lie down.

v.n. To roll over; to roll and toss restlessly; to flounder; to die suddenly; — to be agitated with love, to be in love
v.n. To take a roll; to roll on the ground.

subs: 'To take up a (or the) loṭā'; to perform menial services (for)

more: s. m. 1. (see لوٿنا loṭnā ) A tumbling. 2. A kind of pigeon, tumbler. 3. A bush.

v.n. (intens.) To roll; to lie (or throw oneself) down and turn or roll over (as a horse, or a child in a temper); to roll over, to fall down, to be brought down (as an animal that has been shot, &c.); to lie down, to be beaten down (as a field of corn, &c.); to throw oneself down, to fall down (at, -meṅ, e.g. pairoṅ-meṅ loṭ-gayā); — to long (for), be eager (for or after anything); to die (for); to be agitated with love; to be deeply in love (with); — to be ruined, become bankrupt, to fail (as a firm, e.g. koṭhī loṭ-gaʼī); — to miss, to fail (e.g. lohā loṭ-gayā); — to turn, to change for the worse
v.n. To roll or toss about from restlessness or pain.

ety: lubhā˚ = lubhāw˚ = Prk. लोभावे(इ), fr. S. लोभ+आपि (caus. augment)
def: v.t. To excite desire in, to make (one) long (for, -par), to tantalize, to cause (one) to covet; to allure, attract (by), entice, tempt, seduce; to draw or win over; to fascinate, charm; — v.n. To desire greatly or eagerly, to long (for, -par), to covet; to be allured or tempted (by, -par; syn. lubdha honā)
more: v. a. S. लुभाना 1. To allure; entice; charm; seduce ( rijhānā , 2). 1. Woh jogan bhī sau sau tarah kar adā, Har ek ān meṅ usko letī lubhā . Mīr Hasan. 2. Mathwā de bundā, lubhāve das gunḍā . Prov. With spangles on her forehead she lures ten lecherous men. 2. To draw or win over. lubhā'o , W. Rus.; maṅgnī , E. n. m. Over and above ( rūkan ).
v. a. To excite desire, tantalize, allure, entice. See للچانا lalchānā.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لُغَت (luġat), from Arabic لُغَة (luḡa). First attested in 1707 as Middle Hindi لغت (lġt /⁠luġat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of lug̠at, q.v.), Tongues, languages, &c.; forms, modes of writing and pronouncing words.
more: n. f. Gr. logos , pl. of لغت word. A dictionary; lexicon ( kosh ). lugat tarāshnā, yā ghaṛnā , v. n. To coin big words. lugat-tarāshī , Big words; pedantry. lugat jhāṛnā , v. a. To use big words.
s. plur. of لغت lug̠ẖat, q. v.

def: s.m. (rustic) A man, a male (cf. lugāʼī).
s.m. = lugṛā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), Ragged or worn-out cloth; tatter; old clothes; (rustic) garment, wrapper, mantle, sheet (syn. lūgṛā, lokrā, kapṛā).
adj. Belonging to language; — verbal, literal
more: adj. Verbal; literal. lugvī mānī ; H. akshar-ārth . Primary meaning.
adj. Literal, verbal. لغوي معني lug̠ẖawī maﻌnī, Literal or verbal meaning.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لغوی (luġawī), from Arabic لُغَوِيّ (luḡawiyy). Related to لغو (lağv, “nonsense”).

def: s.f. A mass or lump (of some soft or clammy substance, as dough, kneaded earth, &c.); a ball (of anything that has been pounded or brayed, as levigated medicaments, &c.), a pill, a bolus.
more: n. f. luggad , aug. A lump of anything ground (esp. of bhang , etc.). lugdī banānā , v. n. To form into a lump.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), Ragged or worn-out cloth; tatter; old clothes; (rustic) garment, wrapper, mantle, sheet (syn. lūgṛā, lokrā, kapṛā).

n. m. 1. Tattered or worn out cloth; old clothes. 1. Le lugṛī, chal gudṛī! Prov. Old clothes take, for the market make! 2. Nāyā lūgā nau din, lugṛī baris din . Bhoj. Prov. The new dress for nine days display, the old the year round wear each day. 2. ( Rus .) A wrapper; a sheet.
s.f. (dialec.) = lukṭī or lūkṭī, qq.v. s.f. A stake burnt at one end; a brand, &c. ( = lukāṭhī, q.v.); — a firebrand, mischief-maker, tell-tale (also written lūkṭī).

ety: v.n. fr. لغز 'to change; to distort'
def: s.m. An enigma, a riddle; a puzzle; an ambiguous phrase; anything occult, or mysterious, or difficult to be understood; — a labyrinth.
more: (or lug̠ẖaz, from لغز lag̠ẖaza, To distort, Heb. A barbarian, one who uses a foreign or unknown tongue) s. m. Any ambiguous phrase, an enigma, a riddle.
part. act. (of لغزيدن) in compos. Stumbling, slipping, tripping.

def: s.f. A hot wind, &c. ( = luk, lūk, qq.v.). s.f. A fiery phenomenon in the sky; meteor; falling star; firebrand; flame; hot blast (of a furnace, &c.), hot wind (cf. lūk, lū, or lūh).
more: s. m. Iron. لوها بجانا lohā bajānā, To fight with swords. لوه لاٿهـ loha-lāṭh, s. m. An iron mace or club.

def: s.m. Burning, flaming, blazing; flame without smoke; heat (of a fire, &c.).
(from لـﮩب Heb. ) s. m. Blazing, flame without smoke, burning.
(from لوهو lohū, q. v.) adj. Bloody.
adj. = lohān, q.v. adj. Covered with blood, bloodstained, bloody, cruel (com. written luhān; and sometimes lahān).
(from لوهو lohū, q. v.) adj. Bloody.
adj. = lohān, q.v. adj. Covered with blood, bloodstained, bloody, cruel (com. written luhān; and sometimes lahān).
s. m. An iron pot.
s.m. An iron pot.
s. f. A staff armed with iron.
s.f. An iron-bound staff or club; a bludgeon; a crowbar.

n. m. luhāran , n. f. S. लोहकार A blacksmith; a worker in iron. luhār-khāna , n. m. A blacksmith's shop. luhār-khāne meṅ suiyāṅ bechnā, ulṭe bāṅs Barailī ko . To carry coals to Newcastle. luhār kī bhaṭṭī ; Tir. lohārak pasār , n. f. 1. A blacksmith's forge. 2. A furnace. lokh'ar ; Bhoj. lohkhar , n. m. 1. Hardware; tools. 2. Old iron; pots and pans.
s. m. A blacksmith.
s.m. A worker in iron, a blacksmith; a smith
subs: 'To sell needles in a blacksmith's shop,' to carry coals to Newcastle
s.f. A blacksmith's forge; a furnace.

def: s.f. A blacksmith's wife.
s. f. The female of لہار luhār.
s.m. = loh-ćūn, q.v.s.v. loh.

adj. & s.m. = lunj, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame of a hand or hands; lame of hands and feet; without hands; without hands and feet; crippled, lame; — one who has lost the use of his hands, or of his fingers and toes; one who is without hands, or hands and feet; a cripple
s.m. Plenty of water; the great deep; middle of the sea; an abyss
more: (from لجّ) s. m. Deep water, the middle of the sea, an abyss. لجه خيزي lujja-ḵẖez-ī, The agitation or ebullition of the abyss.
subs: s.f. The agitation (or ebullition) of the abyss.

def: s.f. A fiery phenomenon in the sky; meteor; falling star; firebrand; flame; hot blast (of a furnace, &c.), hot wind (cf. lūk, lū, or lūh).
s.m. Plucking out or off, cutting off; dropping out, elision, rejection.
s.f. Flame; fire: blast (of a furnace); hot wind ( = luk, lū, lūh, lūkā, lūkn).
See لوکهـ چلانا under لوکهـ.
s.m. Varnish; a kind of glazing composition; — a coating of varnish, &c.

ety: lūk, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Flame; fire, &c. ( = lūk, q.v.); a piece of burning matter thrown out from a conflagration, burning grass, &c.
more: n. m. S. उल्का, Pr. ukkā . 1. Flame; fire. Tere muṅh ko lūkā lage! A curse. 2. Pieces of burning matter thrown out from a conflagration. lūkā lagānā, yā denā , v. a. See āg lagānā , 2, 6—12. Tohrā mūṅh meṅ lūkā lagāoṅ! kasan bhākhā nikāselā! Bhoj. Wom. lukā'ṭhī, lukṭhī , E. n. f. 1. Burning wood; half-burnt wood. 2. A wooden poker, or stake burnt at one end; a firebrand.
s. m. Pieces of burning matter thrown out from a conflagration. لوکا لگانا lūkā lagānā, To inflame or excite quarrels, &c.
subs: or lūkā lagānā (-meṅ), To put or apply a brand or a torch (to), to set fire (to); — to inflame or excite quarrels (see āg lagānā).

def: adj. & s.m. Vain; roguish; profligate; — a roguish fellow; a scamp; a profligate (syn. luqandrā).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Concealed, hidden, &c.

ety: caus. of luknā; — lukā˚ = luk˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To hide, conceal.
more: Rus. v. a. S. लुक to disappear. To hide ( chhupānā ).
(causal of لکنا) v. a. To hide, to conceal.

def: s.m. = لقندرا luqandrā, q.v. s.m. A scamp, a dissolute fellow, a profligate, a rake, a blackguard; a cheat.

ety: said to be corr. fr. qalandar; but cf. luqqā
def: s.m. A scamp, a dissolute fellow, a profligate, a rake, a blackguard; a cheat.
more: n. m. A transposition of qalandar a devotee. 1. A loose character; a profligate. 2. A scamp; cheat.
s. m. A rake, a profligate.

def: s.m. A kind of ointment, by applying which to a person's eyes he is said to become invisible.
more: s. m. An ointment, by applying which to a manʼs eyes he becomes invisible.

def: s.m. Hiding, concealment.
s.f. A firebrand, &c. ( = lukāṭhī, q.v.). s.f. Burning wood; a brand, a firebrand; half-burnt wood; a stake burnt at one end; a wooden poker.
n. m. Half-burnt wood; a brand.
s.m. = H لوکٿي लूकटी lūkṭī, s.f. = lukṭī, and lukāṭhī, qq.v.
s.f. Burning wood; a brand, a firebrand; half-burnt wood; a stake burnt at one end; a wooden poker.
v.t. = lukānā, q.v. v.t. To hide, conceal.
s.f. Flame, &c. ( = lūk, lūkā, qq.v.)
subs: (or lūk) ćalānā, To stir or blow up a flame; (jig.) to charm, fascinate.

def: s. f. A flame. لوکهـ چلانا lūkh chalānā, To charm, to fascinate.

ety: for A. لقمة luqmat, n. of unity fr. لقم 'to swallow, to gobble'
def: s.m. A mouthful, morsel, bit, scrap, sop
more: n. m. A morsel; mouthful; bit; sop. luqmā denā , v. a. 1. To put words in one's mouth. 2. To interrupt one while he is speaking. 3. To feed the fire. luqmah k. , v. a. To gulp down; swallow.
s. m. A morsel, a mouthful.
subs: (-ko), To give a morsel (to); — to put words into the mouth (of); to put scraps (into); to feed (a fire); to interrupt (a person while he is speaking)
(-ko), To make a mouthful of; to swallow, to gulp down
s.f. A bigoted or superstitious prejudice against certain common articles of food (entertained by some as a token of great piety and devotion).

ety: luk˚ = Prk. लुक्क(इ); prob. fr. S. लुप्+कृ
def: v.n. To hide, to lie hidden; to conceal oneself; to disappear, to be or become invisible
more: v. n. To hide oneself; disappear; to be invisible. Nāhnā nāhnā kātūṅ, rī māṅ, sahaj sahaj aṭerūṅ, rī māṅ! Sāsre nā jāūṅ, rī māṅ, chākī, tale luk jāūṅ, rī māṅ! Wom. Song. (Sung when the husband comes to take away his child-wife). Mother! I'll spin the thread so fine, and carefully I 'll reel, I'll go not to my father-in-law's, I'll hide beneath the mill.
v. n. To lie hid, to be concealed.
v. a. (dakh.) To write: see لکهنا likhnā.

def: v.t. To look at, to see, behold.
v.n. To be scorched by the sun; to be struck by the blast (of a furnace), or by a hot wind; to have a coup-de-soleil.
more: v. a. To catch. ¶ lūknā, v. n. To be scorched by the sun or hot wind, to have a coup de soleil.

ety: for A. لکنة, inf. n. of لکن 'to pronounce or speak barbarously; to stammer,' &c.
def: s.f. Stammering, stuttering; impediment in speech (syn. haklānā, haklāpan); a lisp; speaking indistinctly (from the effects of paralysis, or of intoxication, &c.).
more: n. f. لکن to stammer. 1. Speaking thick (as a man in liquor); speaking indistinctly, from the as a man in effects of paralysis, etc. 2. Stuttering; stammering.
(from لکن To speak a barbarous language) s. f. Stuttering, lisp, stammering.

def: adj. & s.m. Stammering, &c.; — a stammerer, stutterer; lisper; one who has an impediment in his speech.
s.m. corr. of nuqṣān, q.v. s.m. Defect; deficiency; loss; waste; detriment, injury, harm, damage; blemish; prejudice; mischief
more: (for نقصان nuḳṣān, q. v.) s. (dakh). Injury, defect, detriment, &c.
subs: s.m. Voluntary, or intentional, waste or damage
v.n. To bear a loss (i.q. nuqṣān uṭhānā)
adj. Receiving or suffering diminution, or harm, or injury, or loss; — nuqṣān pahuṅćānā (-ko), To cause loss, damage, or injury (to)
or nuqṣān-ě-ḵẖāṣ, s.m. Personal harm or injury
s.f. Causing harm, injury, or loss (to); doing a mischief (to)
(-kā), To do harm or injury (to), to affect injuriously; — to destroy, ruin
To put up with (or to abide) a loss, &c.

ety: also lukṭhī, contrac. of lukāṭhī, q.v.; or = luk, q.v.+Prk. अडिआ = S. ट ( = र)+इका
def: s.f. A stake burnt at one end; a brand, &c. ( = lukāṭhī, q.v.); — a firebrand, mischief-maker, tell-tale (also written lūkṭī).

ety: prob. for lukṭī, rt. of luk-nā, q.v.+Prk. अडिआ = S. ट ( = र)+इका
def: s.f. A fox (syn. lokṛī or lokhṛī, lomṛī).
s.f. = H لوکٿ लूकट lūkaṭ, s.m. = lukṭī, and lukāṭhī, qq.v.
more: s. f. 1. A wooden poker, or stake burnt at one end. 2. A fox.

ety: prop. lukwāʼī; fr. lūk, q.v. + wāʼī or awāʼī = Prk. आव or आवे+ अइआ = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+ता+इका (तिका)
def: s.f. Conflagration (syn. agwāhī, dāh).
s. f. Conflagration.

ety: prop. lukwāʼī; fr. lūk, q.v. + wāʼī or awāʼī = Prk. आव or आवे+ अइआ = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+ता+इका (तिका)
def: s.f. Conflagration (syn. agwāhī, dāh).

ety: Prk. लूरओ, fr. rt. लूर˚ (said to be a substitute for S. छिद्); prob. fr. S. लू, or from लुल्
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame (of the hands); without hands; lame; crippled; withered or distorted (a limb); paralysed; — one who has lost the use of hands and feet; a cripple.
more: adj. 1. E. Without hands. 2. W. Lame.

def: s.f. A dancing-girl, a courtezan, strumpet; — also = P. lūlū, q.v.
more: s. f. 1. See P. لولو. 2. (in Hind.) ¶ lolī, A courtezan, prostitute. لولي فلک lolī-i-falak, The planet Venus.

def: s.m. A pearl (pl. laʼālī).
more: n. m. 1. A goblin to frighten children; a bugaboo. 2. A madman; a fool.
s. m. A pearl. (Plur. لالي laʼālī ).
s. m. 1. A people in Persia, called also Kārājī. 2. A goblin to frighten children. ( Burh. ḳāt̤. ).

def: s.m. A figure dressed up (or a goblin) to frighten children, a bugaboo; a madman; a fool; — name of a handsome people in Persia (called also Karaji).
s.m. A paw.
adj. Of or relating to pearls; pearly; pearl-coloured; — s.m. A pearl-merchant.
s.m. Meanness, baseness, avarice; vice.
s.m. A tail; a hairy tail (as a horse's, or monkey's, &c.).

ety: lūm˚, prob. = S. लम्ब; cf. lom, 'base'
def: v.n. (rustic) To depend, hang low, lower (as clouds).
more: v. n. To lower (clouds). Lūm lūm dhartī se lāge, būṅd nahīṅ, lārī, Kit uṭh gaë, Dīnā nāth? baras ke bhar de kyārī! Rus. The clouds bend down to earth, no drop of rain doth fall, God of the poor where gone! with rain my fields fill all!

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Cut, &c. ( = lūna or lūn, q.v.). part. (f. -ā), Cut, severed, clipped; reaped; plucked, gathered (as flowers, &c.); cut off; cut down; wounded, destroyed.
part. (f. -ā), Cut, severed, clipped; reaped; plucked, gathered (as flowers, &c.); cut off; cut down; wounded, destroyed.

ety: prob. S. लण्ढ+कः; or लुण्ड ( = लुण्ट)+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Tail-less, tail-cropt; bob-tailed; docked, &c. ( = lāṅḍā, and lanḍūrā, qq.v.).
s.m. A lump, a round ball of (anything); a ring of twisted grass or twine (laid under a round-bottomed jar to prevent it from oversetting: — syn. jūnā).

adj. 1. Tailcropt; bobtailed ( bāṅḍā ). 2. Stripped (of leaves and branches). 3. Without friends; forlorn. 4. See jūnā. lunḍ munḍ , adj. Tailless and bald ( lunḍā , 2.). Pat-jhaṛ āī aur dar/?/kht lunḍ munḍ hue. lunḍ munḍ k. , v. a. To strip; make a clean sweep.
v.t. = luṛhānā, q.v.
v. a. 1. To spill (a fluid). 2. To overturn.
v.n. = لڙهنا luṛhnā, q.v. v.n. To roll, &c. ( = luṛhaknā, q.v.)
subs: roll over, roll down, topple over, fall down; to slip or slide down; — to recline, lie down, take rest; — to be spilt
v.n. (intens.) To roll over, &c.; to drop or fall down; to fall down dead.

def: v.n. To roll.
adj. (dakh.) Tail-less and bald.
adj. Tail-less and bald; bare, stripped
subs: v.t. To make bare, to strip; to make a clean sweep of or in.

ety: prob. = S. लिङ्ग; cf. langoṭ; also lugṛā
def: s.m. A kind of dhotī (q.v.), a cloth worn round the loins and passed between the legs, the end being tucked in behind (it differs from the langoṭ in extending down to the knees)
more: s. m. A cloth worn round the loins with the end passed between the thighs and tucked in behind, or a cloth worn between the legs. لنگ مارنا lung mārnā, To tie on the لنگ q. v. ¶ lang, adj. Lame. (s. लंग Lameness).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀮𑀺𑀁𑀕 (liṃga), from Sanskrit लिङ्ग (liṅga).

ety: prob. lung, q.v.+ārā = ālā = Prk. आलओ = S. आल+ कः
def: adj. Dissolute, profligate, lewd, lecherous; — s.m. A dissolute fellow, a profligate, a libertine (cf. laṅgar, laṅgarwā).
more: n. m. A dissolute or abandoned wretch; a profligate; rip; loose fish ( badm/?/āsh ).

ety: prob. lung, q.v.+ārā = ālā = Prk. आलओ = S. आल+ कः
def: adj. Dissolute, profligate, lewd, lecherous; — s.m. A dissolute fellow, a profligate, a libertine (cf. laṅgar, laṅgarwā).
s.m. Dissolute conduct, profligacy, libertinism; rascality, villainy.
n. m. 1. Profligacy; libertinism (بدمعاشي). 2. Rascality; villainy.
s.m. Dissolute conduct, profligacy, libertinism; rascality, villainy.
s.f. 1˚ = lung, q.v.; — 2˚ A coloured cloth worn (by Moḥammadans) as a lung or dhotī — 3˚ A turban with a check (or gold) border.
more: n. f. Cor. of luṅg . 1. A colored check worn by Mah. as a dhotī , q. v. 2. A turban with a check or gold border.
s. f. See لنگ lung, or it is a coloured cloth worn as the lung, q. v.

ety: lūn, q.v.+rā = Prk. डओ (with ग inserted) = S. र+कः
def: adj. (dialec.) Salt, saline, brackish.
more: adj. Salt, brackish. ¶ laungrā, s. m. Name of a medicine.

def: s.f. Cutting, reaping; — rice.
s.f. Cutting, reaping; cutting off; — rice.
s.f. Butter.

ety: lūn, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The salt that effloresces from walls, &c.
more: s. f. 1. (from लवण ) The salt that effloresces from walls, &c. 2. (for नवनीत ) Butter.

def: adj. (dakh.) Lame: see لنج lunj.

ety: prob. S. लुिज्जतः, rt. लुञ्ज् = लञ्ज्;; or लून+पञ्जक;; Pers. also lunj and lunja
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame of a hand or hands; lame of hands and feet; without hands; without hands and feet; crippled, lame; — one who has lost the use of his hands, or of his fingers and toes; one who is without hands, or hands and feet; a cripple
more: adj. Lame of a hand or hands, having lost the use of hands and feet.

ety: prob. S. लुिज्जतः, rt. लुञ्ज् = लञ्ज्;; or लून+पञ्जक;; Pers. also lunj and lunja
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame of a hand or hands; lame of hands and feet; without hands; without hands and feet; crippled, lame; — one who has lost the use of his hands, or of his fingers and toes; one who is without hands, or hands and feet; a cripple
more: adj. Cont. of lūn panjā . 1. (W.) Without hands. 2. (E.) Without hands and feet; crippled.
adj. Lame of a hand or hands, having lost the use of hands and feet.

ety: prob. S. लुिज्जतः, rt. लुञ्ज् = लञ्ज्;; or लून+पञ्जक;; Pers. also lunj and lunja
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Lame of a hand or hands; lame of hands and feet; without hands; without hands and feet; crippled, lame; — one who has lost the use of his hands, or of his fingers and toes; one who is without hands, or hands and feet; a cripple
more: adj. Lame of a hand or hands, having lost the use of hands and feet.

def: v.t. See لننا lunnā (the usual form).

ety: lun˚ = Prk. लुण(इ) = S. लुना(ति), rt. लू
def: v.t. To cut, to reap.
more: v. a. To reap: see لونا launā.

def: adj. = luṇḍā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Tail-less, tail-cropt; bob-tailed; docked, &c. ( = lāṅḍā, and lanḍūrā, qq.v.).
s.m. A sort of pot-herb, Chironia centauroides (common in cultivated fields after the rains (ʻThe whole plant is powerfully bitter, and is held in great repute as a tonic by the natives').
s.f. (dialec.) Hiding through fear; fear, alarm, terror
n. f. ( Contemp ). A cap.
s.f. = lupṛī, q.v.
more: n. f. S. लोत्प्री or लिप to cover, plaster. 1. A poultice; a plaster. 2. Flour, plaster, etc. shaped like a cap, and hence applied contemptuously to a cap or turban ( lupp/?/o ), whence the diminutive lupṛī . 3. (Wom.) An old ragged cap or turban. Jale kī bhare bazār meṅ lipṛī utār lūṅgī! Wom. 4. Covering for the body; clothing ( kapṛā ). Lipṛī, batānā, chor sab le līyā, ek bishṭī de dīyā . E. The thieves took my turban and all my clothes, and left me only a strip for my loins. lupṛī bāṅdhnā , v. n. To apply a poultice.

ety: S. लोप्त्री, rt. लुप्; or perhaps a form of lipṛī
def: s.f. A lump of anything moist; a lump of dough; ḥalwā made of wheat flour (with little butter or ghī); — pap, &c. ( = lapsī); — a poultice, plaster ( = lopṛī)
part. Robbed, plundered, deprived of; omitted, left off, neglected; disused, obsolete; suppressed, cut off, elided, dropped, removed; disappeared, lost, gone, extinct; — s.m. Plunder, booty.
s.f. The state of being cut off or elided; suppression; disappearance; non-existence.
s.m. (dialec.) Sense, understanding.
more: Bhoj. n. m. Understanding. Lūr na ūr, chalā miyāṅ Jagdīspūr . Prov. Nor sense nor skill he shows, to Jagdispur he goes.

def: v.n. To roll, to roll over, roll off, fall off; to tip or topple over; to slip, slide
more: v. n. To roll, to fall (or roll) off, to slip, to slide.

def: v.n. = luṛakhnā, q.v. v.n. To roll, to roll over, roll off, fall off; to tip or topple over; to slip, slide

v. n. 1. To roll; roll down; move or slide down. Jahāṅ dekheṅ gunnā, pūrī, tahāṅ jāëṅ luṛhī luṛhī . Prov. Wherever cakes and sweets he spies thither sliding soon he hies. 2. To take rest; lie down. Do ghaṛī luṛhak raho . 3. To be spilt. 4. ( Slang ) To drop off; drop down dead. luṛhtā puṛhtā , Rolling and tumbling down. Luṛtā puṛtā loṭā āyā, Banno ke ghar beṭā āyā . Song. Ṭesū .
v.n. To roll, roll over, roll down, topple over, fall down; to slip or slide down; — to recline, lie down, take rest; — to be spilt
more: v. n. To roll, to be spilt. لڙه جانا luṛh jānā, To die.
subs: v.n. (intens.) To roll over, &c.; to drop or fall down; to fall down dead.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa लुढइ (luḍhaï) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓𑁆𑀓- (-kka-), from Prakrit 𑀮𑀼𑀠𑀇 (luḍhaï), from Sanskrit लुठति (luṭháti).

def: v. a. Caus. of لرَهنا q. v.
s.f. contrac. of loṛhiyā, the dim. of loṛhā, qq.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. लुठिता, rt. लुठ्
def: v.t. To hem (a garment); to seam.
more: v. a. To double hem a garment.

def: s.f. Hemming; seaming.
v.t. To cause to roll, to set a-rolling; to cause to roll down, or roll over; to cause to topple over; to upset; to spill; to cause to slip or slide.
